data.title: New Zealand home prices dip in January, holiday lull hits demand
data.description:
New Zealand home prices dipped in January as seasonal holiday patterns dampened demand and unusual severe weather across parts of the North Island disrupted activity, the Real Estate Institute of New Zealand (REINZ) said on Monday.

Seasonally adjusted median house prices fell 1.2% from December, but nudged 0.4% higher on the previous year, REINZ data showed. National home sales slumped 8.9% from December and were 0.6% lower from January 2025.

"Interpreting the market over the summer can be challenging, as activity between November and February often reflects seasonal patterns rather than fundamental shifts," REINZ Chief Executive Lizzy Ryley said in a statement.

Ryley said once seasonal trends were taken into account, prices remained broadly stable in January, suggesting buyer confidence was rebuilding gradually rather than accelerating quickly.

data.title: BlueScope 1H Net Profit More Than Doubles; Hikes Dividend
data.description:
(20:35 GMT) *BlueScope Steel 1H Net Profit A$390.8 Million Vs A$179.1 Million Year Earlier
(20:37 GMT) *BlueScope Interim Dividend 65 Australian Cents/Share
(20:37 GMT) *BlueScope Targeting Shareholder Returns of A$3.00/Share in 2026
(20:38 GMT) *BlueScope Targeting A$1.30/Share Annual Ordinary Dividend
(20:39 GMT) *BlueScope Intends to Spend A$310 Million on Buyback, Or Other Returns if Share Repurchases Limited
(20:40 GMT) *BlueScope 1H Sales Revenue From Continuing Ops A$8.22 Billion, Up 4% On-Year
(20:40 GMT) *BlueScope 1H Underlying Profit A$382.0 Million Vs A$176.4 Million Year Prior
(20:41 GMT) *BlueScope 1H Underlying Ebitda A$915.3 Million, Up 39% On-Year
(20:42 GMT) *BlueScope 1H Underlying Ebit A$557.5 Million, Up 81% On-Year
(20:42 GMT) *BlueScope Net Debt A$2.2 Million at Dec. 31
(20:43 GMT) *BlueScope Expects 2H Underlying Ebit of A$620 Million-A$700 Million
(20:44 GMT) *BlueScope Expects Lower 2H Result From Australia Business Vs 1H
(20:44 GMT) *BlueScope Expects 15% Higher 2H Result From North America Business Vs 1H
(20:45 GMT) *BlueScope Expects a One-Third Lower 2H Result From Asia Business Vs 1H
(20:46 GMT) *BlueScope: Recorded Net Annualized Cost Improvements of A$190 Million in 1H
(20:47 GMT) *BlueScope: Expects Full Benefits of Further A$150M Cost Savings in FY 2027
(20:48 GMT) *BlueScope: Will Now Target Net Debt of Up to A$1.5 Billion; Will Surpass as Needed
(21:11 GMT) BlueScope 1H Net Profit More Than Doubles; Hikes Dividend

By Rhiannon Hoyle

BlueScope Steel on Monday said its first-half net profit more than doubled and that it will increase dividend payments and intends to buy back more stock, after pledging to increase returns to shareholders.

The Australian steelmaker, which recently rebuffed a takeover bid by conglomerate SGH and U.S. steelmaker Steel Dynamics, said it is targeting shareholder returns totaling 3.00 Australian dollars a share (US$2.12) in 2026.

That includes a 65-Australian cent interim dividend declared by directors for its first fiscal half, up from 30 Australian cents a year ago. BlueScope said it will target an annual ordinary dividend of A$1.30 a share.

It also aims to spend A$310 million on an on-market share buy-back, or some other return method if necessary. The company already announced a A$1.00 a share special dividend last month.

BlueScope's new chief executive, Tania Archibald, recently outlined plans to make more cost savings and boost shareholder returns, after BlueScope last month rejected the roughly US$8.8 billion takeover plan by SGH and Steel Dynamics.

The steelmaker on Monday said it made a net profit of A$390.8 million in the six months through December, up from A$179.1 million in the year-prior period.

BlueScope reported underlying earnings before interest and taxes of A$557.5 million, up 81% year over year. It forecast a second-half result of between A$620 million and A$700 million.

Write to Rhiannon Hoyle at rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com
